title: Eyes on Arts
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.

Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts. These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis. Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 11 April


Theatre Sports Evening (19:00) at the Market Community Theatre. This fun event is hosted by Eden Theatre Community.

About theatre sports: fast-paced, hilarious improvisation games in which actors create scenes on the spot, guided by audience suggestions. No scripts, just quick thinking and big laughs.

Performers and audiences are welcome. Entrance is free, but any donations are appreciated to help with the ongoing improvement and development of the theatre. Contact Stretch on 076 981 4066.


> 11 April


Hallelujah! (19:00) with Carpe Musicam! Orchestra & Choir at Laerskool George-Suid. Tickets are R150 (adults) or R50 (pupils). For tickets and more info, visit www.carpemusicam.co.za.


> 12 April


Various artists live (13:00 to 16:00) at Girls on the Square, 1 Owen Grant St, Wilderness. Relax in a cosy, African-inspired atmosphere highlighted by stunning artwork by local artist Peter Pharoah. For more information, contact 044 877 0445 or email [email protected].


> 13 April


Around the World in Eighty Minutes (16:30) at the DR Mother Church. The George Music Society (GMS) will host the brilliant saxophone quartet Quartuor Avena from France. Tickets costs R170 for public, R50 for learners and R120 for GMS members, and are available at the concert and at www.georgemusicsociety. The musicians include Adam Campbell (South Africa), Sumika Tsujimoto (Japan), Fabio Cesare (Italy) and Nicolas Allard (France).

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
